verses.text: 'Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O LORD,' he prays, begging God's ears be attentive. From the lowest place he calls. No depth of trouble or guilt is too deep for the cry of faith to reach God.

'Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O LORD,' he prays, begging God's ears be attentive. From the lowest place he calls. No depth of trouble or guilt is too deep for the cry of faith to reach God.

'If thou, LORD, shouldest mark iniquities... who shall stand? But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.' None could survive strict reckoning. The wonder of pardon is what draws the heart to reverence and love.

'If thou, LORD, shouldest mark iniquities... who shall stand? But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.' None could survive strict reckoning. The wonder of pardon is what draws the heart to reverence and love.

'I wait for the LORD... and in his word do I hope,' longing for Him 'more than they that watch for the morning.' His hope rests on the promise. The waiting soul looks for God as the weary watchman looks for dawn.

'I wait for the LORD... and in his word do I hope,' longing for Him 'more than they that watch for the morning.' His hope rests on the promise. The waiting soul looks for God as the weary watchman looks for dawn.

Let Israel hope in the LORD, for 'with him is plenteous redemption,' and 'he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.' Pardon is full and free. The God who forgives one sinner has redemption enough for all His people's sins.

Let Israel hope in the LORD, for 'with him is plenteous redemption,' and 'he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.' Pardon is full and free. The God who forgives one sinner has redemption enough for all His people's sins.
